review_scorecard.confidence_note: Confidence reflects how clear and well-supported the deterministic interpretation is, not investment conviction.

categories.explanation: Guidance language is present, but its direction is not explicit enough to score strongly.
strongest_evidence: in Q4, growing 65% year over year for
$70 billion. Backlog grew by 55% quarter over quarter to $240
categories.name: Guidance Strength

categories.explanation: Management confidence blends reassurance cues (high) with uncertainty and Q&A follow-through.
strongest_evidence: [well positioned] We are really well positioned going
[well positioned] round to date and is well positioned
categories.name: Management Confidence

categories.explanation: Management language includes repeated hedging or visibility caveats, which raises uncertainty.
strongest_evidence: [subject to] based on current expectations and assumptions that are subject to a
[modal uncertainty] Actual results could differ materially.
categories.name: Uncertainty / Hedging

categories.explanation: Analyst questions show some pushback or follow-up pressure, but not a full escalation.
strongest_evidence: [where are you on that] And then second, on monetization, where are you on that?
[could you just talk about] Could you just talk a little bit about how you align
categories.name: Analyst Skepticism

categories.explanation: Insufficient Q&A contrast evidence was available, so pressure shift stays neutral.
categories.name: Q&A Pressure Shift

categories.explanation: Q&A evidence is unavailable, so answer directness stays neutral by design.
